behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler wiring: raw-body signature check plus no enqueue side effect. MOCK-MISS: this is not just hash-function math; it can fail at handoff between HTTP parsing, verifier, and queue call. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process HTTP + real middleware stack is sufficient. Rejected higher level (System) because behavior text only requires 401 + no forward, not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Start the app in-process, send an HTTP request with a tampered body/signature pair, assert `401`, and assert queue `enqueue` was not called (or queue table unchanged). Use real request-body extraction path used in production middleware.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (handler -> queue persistence -> response). MOCK-MISS: unit-testing handler alone can miss wrong await/order. REAL-INFRA: no external network/OS primitive is required beyond local app + persistence module. Rejected System because behavior text is about sequencing with the persistent queue, not real remote infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP test against the real queue persistence adapter (e.g., local test DB/file store). Instrument timestamps or hooks to verify queue write commit occurs before `200` is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is async orchestration between request handler and downstream worker kickoff. MOCK-MISS: unit-level function tests can miss promise chaining mistakes across modules. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with in-process slow worker stub; no real OS/network needed. Rejected System because behavior text targets wait semantics, not real network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process server with downstream processor intentionally delayed (e.g., promise resolves after several seconds). Assert HTTP response completes quickly while processor still running, then completes later.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA dominates: this is wall-clock SLA under concurrency and backlog, which depends on real runtime scheduling, socket handling, and timing. In-process mocks can hide event-loop starvation or connection handling issues (MOCK-HIDE).
behaviors.test_description: Launch the service process, create concurrent load with earlier slow events in flight, send a fresh webhook, and measure end-to-end response latency over real HTTP; fail if latency exceeds 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Idempotency bug appears at module/data boundary (handler + dedupe lookup/constraint + queue write), not pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: local persistence integration is enough; no real network/subprocess required. Rejected System because behavior text is duplicate-ID write prevention, which can be validated with local in-process persistence wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the HTTP handler; verify first request is accepted, second does not create an additional queue record (row count unchanged / unique constraint path hit).

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is contract mapping across modules: queue-full signal to HTTP status/header. Unit tests can miss integration of error typing/translation. REAL-INFRA check: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; deterministic full-condition from queue adapter is sufficient. Rejected System because text explicitly says 'queue reports it is full' (already-signaled condition), not real infrastructure saturation behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue adapter/test double to return a `QueueFull` condition from the real handler path; assert HTTP `503` and `Retry-After` header value are present and correct.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This tests queue implementation with real persistence component interactions (capacity accounting + write rejection). MOCK-HIDE: mocked queue would trivially pass and miss real enforcement bugs. Integration brake: no explicit requirement for external process/network; local persistence-backed queue suffices. Rejected System because behavior text does not require remote queue service or OS-level primitives, only actual queue enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against test storage with a small capacity, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one extra and assert deterministic rejection plus unchanged persisted count.
